regions.text: Writing started as record-keeping. The first great poems, like the Iliad, were spoken and memorized long before being written.
The teachings of Buddha, Confucius, and others were oral first. Socrates and Jesus taught through words and examples, not writing.
(Illustration: A small seated figure representing a teacher or spiritual icon).
Later, the written word became the main way humans tried to understand themselves and the world. poet: a maker of poems.
